meta.note: South Africa Stratum B batch 3 — liberation/political and foreign anti-apartheid PUBLISHED BOOKS. Pamphlets, leaflets, periodicals and banned-organisation ephemera deliberately excluded (per scope). Deduped against existing ZA records. Held for lack of a title-specific source: Mandela's No Easy Walk to Freedom (ZA ban low-confidence), and the author-level-only liberation memoirs (Luthuli, Mbeki, Roux, Bernstein, etc.). Held for source conflict: Mzala, To Every Birth Its Blood (McDonald marks 'Passed'), The Marabi Dance (borderline). Sources: theliteraturepolice.com (McDonald), SciELO, PEN/Sunday Times, HSF, Wikipedia(+LA Times).

entries.title: If This Be Treason
entries.description_ban: Joseph's diary of the 1956–61 Treason Trial; banned and unavailable in South Africa, finally published there only in 1998 (Kwela 'Pocket Revolutionary' reissue).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of a published book; documented by the Sunday Times / Kwela.

entries.title: The Rise of the South African Reich
entries.description_ban: Penguin African Library study of the affinities between Afrikaner Nationalism and Nazism; banned in South Africa, with copies smuggled in as samizdat.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of a published book; documented by the Helen Suzman Foundation.

entries.title: Naught for Your Comfort
entries.description_ban: Anglican priest Trevor Huddleston's first-hand account of the Sophiatown removals and apartheid; banned in South Africa while selling some 250,000 copies abroad.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of a published book; documented by the Trevor Huddleston Memorial Centre (medium confidence; no Gazette ref).

entries.title: The Discarded People
entries.description_ban: Catholic priest Cosmas Desmond's exposé of apartheid forced removals (full title 'The Discarded People: An Account of African Resettlement in South Africa'); soon banned in South Africa, and Desmond himself placed under house arrest.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of a published book; documented via Wikipedia with citation.

entries.title: House of Bondage
entries.description_ban: Ernest Cole's photographic indictment of Black urban life under apartheid; declared undesirable by proclamation in the Government Gazette of 10 May 1968, conditionally unbanned in 1990.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban with a Government Gazette date; documented in a peer-reviewed journal (Image & Text, SciELO).

entries.title: Nelson Mandela: The Man and the Movement
entries.description_ban: Mandela biography by the exiled South African-born anti-apartheid writer Mary Benson; banned in apartheid South Africa upon its publication.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of a published book; documented via Wikipedia citing the Los Angeles Times.
entries.source_name: Wikipedia (Mary Benson), citing Los Angeles Times

entries.title: The Park and Other Stories
entries.description_ban: Collection of resistance short stories; the original edition was banned, later cleared in the 1983 BLAC edition. Distinct from Matthews's Cry Rage! and Pass Me a Meatball, Jones.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; listed in McDonald's Literature Police Staffrider/Webnote table (medium confidence).
